_doc: Residential service-page hero image prompts. Routed via OpenRouter -> google/gemini-2.5-flash-image (nano banana). 1200x900 JPEG, ≤300KB.

prompts.prompt: A photorealistic angled close-up of a Texas suburban two-story home's freshly installed dimensional architectural asphalt shingle roof in slate-gray tone, with crisp ridge cap, drip edge, and aluminum gutters visible at the eave. Late afternoon golden-hour light. Mature oak tree branches frame the upper corner. Wide editorial photography aesthetic. No humans, no text, no readable house numbers.

A photorealistic before/after split-frame architectural photograph of a North Texas suburban brick home's full roof replacement: left half shows aged, weather-worn 3-tab asphalt shingles with curling edges; right half shows freshly installed dimensional architectural shingles, new flashings, new ridge cap, new pipe boots. Bright midday sun. No humans, no readable signage.

A photorealistic top-down close-up of a North Texas residential asphalt shingle roof showing inspection markings — small chalk circles around three suspected hail-impact bruises on different shingle tabs, a measuring tape draped across the surface, and a clipboard with checklist visible at the bottom of frame. Natural daylight. Documentary roofing-inspection aesthetic. No humans visible.

A photorealistic atmospheric photograph of a Texas residential roofline at dusk after a thunderstorm: dark gray storm clouds receding, a freshly installed bright blue emergency tarp neatly secured over a damaged roof section with sandbags at the corners, water still beading on the shingles below, porch lights glowing warm. Editorial photojournalism aesthetic. No humans visible. No readable text.

A photorealistic angled close-up of a premium Texas Hill Country style home's standing-seam metal roof in deep charcoal-gray, panels running vertically with crisp 1-inch raised seams, snow guards visible, beautiful integration with stone chimney and stucco gable. Strong directional sunlight. Premium residential aesthetic. No humans, no signage.

A photorealistic close-up of a North Texas residential asphalt shingle roof showing visible hail-impact damage: small circular bruises and granule loss on multiple shingle tabs, a damaged aluminum gutter visible at the eave with a large dent, leaves scattered across the surface from the storm. Diffused overcast light revealing the texture. Documentary insurance-claim aesthetic. No humans, no signage.
